behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names both files (src/pages/api/download/attachments/[fileId].ts and src/utils/delivery-worker.ts) and describes the two functions and the expected call signature (file URL and filename, not fileId).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the broken behavior and which approach to use, but doesn't give the exact code or function name — the agent must find getDownloadUrl vs resolveDownloadUrl in delivery-worker.ts.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on import of getDownloadUrl and call signature getDownloadUrl(file.url, file.name), which is the natural API dictated by delivery-worker.ts signatures; alternative correct fixes would share these.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include subprocess invocations of eslint, prettier, and TypeScript parser in addition to source text assertions.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require actual import changes and call-site rewrite; a stub can't satisfy them without performing the fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests verify the import change, call signature change, and preservation of other module exports as described in instruction.md.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments for f2p, p2p, and repo CI checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code fix, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and function names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to investigate but doesn't specify the exact line or exact function name to use; localization within files is required.
